summary: Two targeted substring patches to WIN-061 detail_evidence resolve the SPE (solar proton event) vs geomagnetic storm category conflation flagged in WIN-061.c4 H5. PATCH A reorders paragraph 1 to lead with Pazos et al. 2019 (genuine CME-driven G-class storms at Coeneo, Dst ≲ −70 nT) as primary storm-time enhancement evidence, demotes Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 to supporting 'distinct but related disturbance class' status with SPE explicitly labeled, and adds a mechanistic parenthetical (proton-precipitation mesospheric coupling vs CME-storm electron-precipitation D-region coupling) that pre-empts the defender category-error attack. PATCH B reworks paragraph 2's S&F reference to cite 'cavity-Q observations during high-energy particle precipitation' (specific finding, not storm-time categorization) and adds a cross-reference to Pazos as the genuine-CME-storm enhancement citation. Character delta: +614 (4524 -> 5138). Both before-strings verified unique. The ~22 Hz baseline, Section 2.1 cross-reference, V51.1 falsification-acknowledgement credit, three-dimension framing, and Unscored-vs-observed contradiction sentence are preserved verbatim. TLDRs and detail_verdict_text are not modified (already direction-mixed and consistent with rewritten paragraphs). Scope strictly ISS-1451; ISS-1454 (H_eff averaging) and ISS-1418 (timestamp-forward) remain open for separate EXPs.
